_usage: Wellness-quiz questions for the signage 'Brain Break' panel. Edit + push to update every TV (read over HTTPS). General health facts -- NO PHI. PENDING Clinical/Marketing sign-off. Each item: q=question, a=answer, s=source.
source: Drafted from CDC / NIH-NIDA-NIMH / SAMHSA / Mayo Clinic / USDA consensus -- verify before relying on.

items.q: How many hours of sleep do most adults need each night?
About how much of the adult human body is water?
What simple daily activity can boost mood and lower stress?
items.a: A short walk -- any movement helps.

items.q: What vitamin does your skin help make from sunlight?
What does mindfulness mean?
items.a: Paying attention to the present moment, without judgment.

items.q: Name one benefit of slow, deep breathing.
items.a: It calms the body's stress response.

items.q: True or False: laughter can lower stress hormones.
How many minutes of moderate activity per week do experts recommend?
What is one easy way to support better sleep?
Which nutrient helps build and repair muscle?
Name a benefit of drinking enough water.
True or False: time in nature can improve well-being.
What does a regular gratitude practice tend to improve?
What is the count for a calming box breath?
Which organ consolidates memory while you sleep?
Name a food group rich in fiber.
What is one sign your body needs water?
True or False: connection with others supports recovery.
Name a grounding technique that uses your senses.
items.a: 5-4-3-2-1: name what you see, hear, feel, smell, taste.

items.q: How many servings of fruits and vegetables are commonly recommended daily?
What is a benefit of a steady daily routine in recovery?
How does staying hydrated help your brain?
items.a: It supports focus and a steadier mood.

items.q: True or False: it takes strength to ask for help.
What does the HALT check-in stand for?
For steady energy, is it better to eat balanced meals or skip them?
What is one benefit of journaling?
items.a: It helps process thoughts and emotions.

items.q: True or False: progress in recovery is rarely a straight line.
What is a healthy first step when an urge hits?
items.a: Pause, breathe, and reach out to support.

items.q: How does exercise affect the brain?
How many cups of water a day is a common target?
Name a relaxing activity that does not need a screen.
True or False: small daily wins add up to big change.
What does a morning gratitude list encourage?
What helps the body recover after exercise?
Name one act of healthy self-care.
True or False: taking things one day at a time is okay.
What should fill about half your plate?
Name a stress-reliever you can do anywhere.
How does sunlight help your mood?
items.a: It supports vitamin D and your daily rhythm.

items.q: True or False: talking about feelings can ease them.
What is a benefit of limiting caffeine late in the day?
True or False: hobbies can support recovery and reduce boredom.
What is a healthy way to mark a recovery milestone?
items.a: Acknowledge it and share it with your support.

items.q: How long of a screen-free wind-down helps before bed?
Name a kind act that also lifts your own mood.
True or False: setbacks can be part of growth.
Name a quick grounding tip for a tough moment.
items.a: Feel your feet on the floor and breathe.

items.q: What keeps motivation steady over time?
What is one good reason to eat breakfast?
items.a: Steady energy and focus through the morning.

items.q: What is the most important thing to remember in recovery?
items.a: You are not alone -- help is always available (call or text 988).
